calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maureen McFadden for and with Mary Doyle (appeared by telephone). Yulissa Zulaica for and with John Reilly. Bridgette Sanders appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Ms. McFadden stated that all of the properties have been sold and that each Beneficiary received $975,000.00 in distribution. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the 1% guideline fee of $35,000.00 paid to Ms. Doyle, the Trustee not being able to provide timesheets for said fee, the $105.00 per hour rate the Trustee is requesting, the property being rented below fair market value, the $1,200.00 payment to Ms. Sanders for travel expenses, the Trustee's failure to insure the Sacramento property, reimbursement of attorney fees for Mr. Reilly, etc. Ms. McFadden is to file a declaration/time itemization regarding Mr. Riordan's fee of $15,000.00 by May 30, 2014. The Petition for Instructions is under submission as of that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Continued To Jan-21-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maureen McFadden for and with Mary Doyle. Yulissa Zulaica for and with John Reilly. Bridget Sanders appeared by telephone in Propria Persona. Michael Reilly in Propria Persona.

Ms. McFadden stated that she is proposing all properties be sold, that her Client has power of sale, and that her Client will list and sell the properties without Court confirmation. Ms. Zulaica stated that she objects to sale of the properties, that the Beneficiaries were asked which property they wanted, that some of the properties are income producing, that her Client indicated that he wanted the Irving Street property and that he obtained an appraisal. Ms. Zulaica also stated that the Trustee flipped her position when her Client asked about valuation of the property. Ms. McFadden stated that another Beneficiary also wants the property. Ms. Zulaica stated that her Client can purchase the property but that he would have to receive his distribution. Michael Reilly stated that he is not willing to mediate or proceed other than by way of sale. Ms. McFadden stated that she would like the Court to rule on the petition regarding sale of the properties, proposed that they amend the accounting to bring it up to October 31, 2013 and continue settlement of the account. The Court bifurcated the issue of sale of the properties (said issue was taken under submission). Settlement of the account and distribution of assets was continued to January 21, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an amended accouting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)
